description: Phase DF motion recipes. Three intentional patterns — ONE entrance, ONE scroll-linked/depth effect, ONE hover/reveal — that together satisfy wisdom rule fs-008. Each entry ships a Framer Motion snippet and a plain-CSS fallback so the recipe applies whether or not the generator targets Framer Motion. LLMs copy short concrete blocks reliably; they paraphrase long ones.

recipes.description: Hero headline + support fade in with a 16px rise. 600ms ease-out, once. Use on H1 + subhead + primary CTA only — do NOT animate every element in the hero.
recipes.whenToUse: Every landing page hero (webspa-product-landing archetype). Do NOT stack this with other entrance animations — one entrance per page.

recipes.description: Sticky navbar that compacts after 40px of scroll — reduces padding, tightens type scale, adds a subtle shadow. Communicates that the page has depth without parallax trickery.
recipes.whenToUse: Any marketing page long enough to scroll. Adds depth without parallax. Alternative: `position: sticky` pinning a phone mockup while text scrolls alongside it (pins work well on feature-explainer sections).

recipes.description: Primary CTA lifts 1px on hover with a subtle shadow increase. Communicates affordance without bouncing. Use on ALL primary buttons; secondary buttons stay flat.
recipes.plainCssFallback: (same as snippet — this one is CSS-first intentionally)
recipes.whenToUse: Every primary CTA. Do NOT also add scale(1.05) or color shifts — one property change per hover is enough. Secondary / tertiary buttons get an underline-on-hover only.

budget.rule: Use 2–3 of these recipes per page. Never more. Never fewer than 2.
